abortReason: Test account (mutykovaelena@gmail.com) temporarily locked by storefront security after rapid automated browsing. Remaining 5 tests blocked.

results.title: Text Section - Max Length Enforced (Required Text, 30-Char Limit)
results.notes: Product AGENT-TEST-RING-TXT-CFG-20260327 has maxlength=255 on the text input (HTML attribute), not 30 as expected. The backend maxLength appears to be null, causing PR #2235 fallback to 255. No character counter visible (no X/30 indicator). The 30-char limit enforcement cannot be tested because the configured maxLength is not 30. This may be a test data configuration issue rather than a code bug, OR it indicates that the admin never set maxLength=30 on this product's Text section.
findings: No visible character counter for limit feedback
1-char text accepted; Add to Cart enabled correctly
31-char text accepted without error (because limit is 255)

results.title: Text Section - Whitespace-Only Input Does Not Satisfy Required Validation
results.notes: 5 spaces typed into required text field. Add to Cart remained disabled. Validation message 'Section is required' shown in red. After entering meaningful text 'Happy Birthday', Add to Cart became enabled.

results.title: Text Section - Optional Text Section Allows Empty and Non-Empty Values
results.notes: Gift Box product (AGENT-TEST-GIFT-TXT-CFG-20260324): (1) With 'None' selected (no text), Add to Cart succeeded. (2) With 'Custom option' selected and text entered, Add to Cart succeeded. Both behaviors correct for optional section.

results.title: Text Section - Long Text at 100-Char Boundary Preserved in Cart and Order
results.notes: Gift Box product has maxlength=255 (not 100 as expected). Same null maxLength fallback as CFG-TEXT-001. Cannot test 100-char boundary. Account locked before deeper investigation.

results.title: Text Section - Special Characters and Unicode Preserved Through Cart
results.notes: Text 'Happy Birthday! & Best Wishes - 2026' (with ampersand and em-dash) preserved correctly in cart as 'Selected text: Happy Birthday! & Best Wishes - 2026'. No HTML encoding artifacts. & displayed as & (not &), em-dash displayed correctly.

results.title: Text Section - Text Value Preserved Through Full Checkout to Order History
results.notes: Text 'With Love Always' added to cart for Ring product. Cart Components list shows '1. Selected text: With Love Always'. Text preserved through PDP to cart. Full checkout flow not completed (would create permanent order and account was later locked).

results.title: Text Section - Required Text Quantity Counter Blocked When Field Empty
results.notes: Ring product (AGENT-TEST-RING-TXT-CFG-20260327) does not have a quantity counter/spinbutton on the PDP. The configurable product appears to default to qty=1 without an explicit quantity selector. Test case assumption about quantity counter is invalid for this product.

results.title: Text Section - Add-to-Cart Button Disabled When Required Text Is Empty
results.notes: Add to Cart button confirmed disabled (HTML disabled attribute) when required text field is empty. Button became enabled after typing text. No cart item created when button disabled.

results.title: Text Section - Paste-Bypass Triggers max_length_exceeded i18n Error and Backend Rejection
results.notes: Cannot test as specified because maxLength is 255 (not 30). The test requires maxLength=30 to test the paste-bypass scenario. Account locked before alternative testing.

results.title: Text Section - Null Backend maxLength Falls Back to 255 Storefront Cap
results.notes: Confirmed on Ring product: HTML input maxlength=255. This matches the expected PR #2235 behavior (section.maxLength ?? 255 fallback). The Ring product's backend maxLength appears to be null, and the frontend correctly defaults to 255. Gift Box product also shows maxlength=255, confirming pattern across products.

results.title: Text Section (Conditional Required) - Toast Save with Empty Required Text Field Must Not Persist to Cart
results.notes: Account locked before reaching the wedding cake product URL. Test requires complex multi-step flow.

results.title: File Section - Upload Button Disabled After 5th File Uploaded (Max Limit)
results.notes: 5 files uploaded successfully: Logo1.png (203kB), Netflix-25.jpg (8.4kB), newier.pdf (135kB), Test.xlsx (8.7kB), large_test.png (5MB). Warning message displayed: 'You have uploaded the maximum number of files possible. Further uploads are not possible.' 6th file (newchanel.jpg) was silently rejected - not added to list. After deleting newier.pdf, 4 files remained and warning cleared. Note: Upload button remains clickable (not disabled) at max - protected by server-side rejection and warning message rather than disabled state.
findings: Upload button NOT disabled at max files (UX concern, not functional bug)
Warning message correctly shown at 5 files
6th file silently rejected (not added to list)
Delete file works; warning clears after deletion

results.title: File Section - Selecting None on Optional Section Clears Uploaded Files
results.notes: After uploading 4 files to optional section, clicking 'None' radio button selected it (checked=true). Files still displayed visually in list after selecting None - however, the section state changed to 'None' meaning no file configuration is active. Add to Cart was enabled (optional section with None is valid). The visual persistence of files after None selection is a UX polish issue but not a functional bug since the configuration state is correctly set to None.
findings: Files still visually shown after None (UX concern)
Add to Cart enabled with None selected (correct for optional section)

results.title: File Section - 0-Byte File Rejected on Required Upload Section
results.notes: Account locked before testing. The large_logo.png (0-byte) file exists in test-data/uploads/ for this test.

results.title: Cart Quantity Counter Does Not Increment When Required Section Is Unfilled
results.notes: On the required File section product (physical-1703), Add to Cart was disabled when no file uploaded. This implicitly covers the quantity increment behavior since no cart operations are possible with unfilled required section.

results.title: Add-to-Cart Button Disabled When Required Section Is Unfilled
results.notes: On product 'Hoodie Base product with only File req' (physical-1703), Add to Cart button had disabled HTML attribute when no file was uploaded. After uploading Logo1.png, button became enabled.

results.title: File Section - Multiple Files Upload and Individual Remove
results.notes: Tested as part of CFG-FILE-001. 5 files uploaded individually. newier.pdf deleted individually via X button - only that file removed, other 4 remained intact.

results.title: File Section - Accepted and Rejected File Types Boundary
results.notes: AVIF file (download.avif) rejected with error 'File format is not allowed (24kB)'. Error banner 'Fix file upload errors to continue.' shown. Valid PNG (Logo1.png) accepted. Accepted format list: DOC, RTF, DOCX, TXT, PDF, XLS, XLSX, JPG, PNG, ODT.

results.title: File Section - Max File Size Enforcement (10MB Platform Limit)
results.notes: large_test.png (5MB) accepted successfully. Section text states 'Each file should not exceed 9.5MB' (platform limit is 9.5MB, not 10MB as stated in test case). Video.mp4 test not completed due to account lockout, but 5MB file within limit confirmed working.

results.title: File Section - Re-Upload After Failed Attempt Works Correctly
results.notes: After AVIF rejection, upload control remained functional. Valid Logo1.png uploaded successfully after the failed attempt. No stale error messages persisted. Add to Cart became enabled with valid file.

results.title: CFG-024: Text Parent Section Triggers Child Visibility on Input
results.notes: Account locked before completing test. Initial observation: product page loaded with 'Engraving Line 1' text section visible. Typed single character 'A'. The regex match found 'STYLE PACK' in page text suggesting the section appeared, but could not verify completely as the browser session was lost to account lockout during evaluation.

results.title: CFG-025: File Parent Section Triggers Child Visibility on Upload
results.notes: Account locked before reaching this test.

globalFindings.description: All configurable products with Text sections have maxlength=255 (the PR #2235 null fallback default) instead of specific maxLength values referenced in test cases (30 for Ring, 100 for Gift Box). This suggests the admin has not set explicit maxLength values on these product configuration sections. Test cases CFG-TEXT-001, CFG-TEXT-004, CFG-TEXT-009 assume specific maxLength values that do not match actual product data.

File upload button (Drag and drop area) remains clickable at max file limit (5 files). A warning message is shown, but the button is not disabled. 6th file upload silently fails. Better UX would disable or hide the upload button at max.

Previously uploaded files remain visually displayed in the file list after selecting 'None' radio on optional File section. While the configuration state correctly becomes 'None', the visual persistence of file entries is confusing.

Test account (mutykovaelena@gmail.com) was temporarily locked by storefront security after approximately 16 minutes of automated testing. This blocked the remaining 5 test cases. The lockout message: 'Your account has been temporarily locked. Please try again later.' The lockout redirected to /blocked page.
playwright-chrome MCP server was unavailable (Target page, context or browser has been closed). Testing was conducted using playwright-edge as fallback per error handling rules.

Product URL in test data for CFG Ring Text (CFG-017) is incorrect. Test data says /products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/configurable-hat but actual URL is /products-with-options/configurations/agent-test-config-engraved-ring-20260327. Search was required to locate the product.

consoleErrors.message: Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () @ https://images.netdirector.co.uk/gforces-auto/image/upload/...
consoleErrors.impact: None - external CDN image 404, not related to configurable products
